summary: CNN独家视频显示参议员麦康奈尔一个月前被担架抬上救护车，其健康状况成谜，共和党同僚称不知其生死。以色列警告美国伊朗计划暗杀特朗普，或解释近期飞机更换及美军打击。纽约危楼抢修工作持续。凯莉·詹娜代言Meta AI眼镜引发隐私争议。

vocabulary.en: a light frame for carrying sick or injured people
vocabulary.excerpt: Neighbors saw the senator being loaded into an ambulance on a stretcher.
vocabulary.example_cn: Para tetangga melihat senator itu diangkat ke ambulans dengan tandu.

vocabulary.en: the quality of being open and honest; not hiding anything
vocabulary.excerpt: It's bringing up questions about transparency surrounding his health.
vocabulary.example_cn: Hal ini menimbulkan pertanyaan tentang transparansi kondisi kesehatannya.

vocabulary.en: information about the plans of an enemy or opponent
vocabulary.excerpt: Israel shares intel of an Iran plant -- plan to assassinate President Trump.
vocabulary.example_cn: Israel membagikan intelijen tentang rencana Iran untuk membunuh Presiden Trump.

vocabulary.en: to murder a prominent person, often for political reasons
vocabulary.excerpt: Israel shares intel of an Iran plant -- plan to assassinate President Trump.
vocabulary.example_cn: Israel membagikan intelijen tentang rencana Iran untuk membunuh Presiden Trump.

vocabulary.en: a sudden or unexpected change or exchange
vocabulary.excerpt: Does that explain the plane switcheroo and the renewed strikes by the U.S.?
vocabulary.example_cn: Bisakah ini menjelaskan pertukaran pesawat dan pemulihan serangan AS?

vocabulary.en: bending or collapsing under pressure
vocabulary.excerpt: Urgent work continues to save that buckling building in New York from partial collapse.
vocabulary.example_cn: 紧急工作持续进行，以拯救纽约那栋弯曲变形的建筑免于部分坍塌。

vocabulary.en: a collapse that affects only part of a structure
vocabulary.excerpt: Urgent work continues to save that buckling building in New York from partial collapse.
vocabulary.example_cn: 紧急工作持续进行，以拯救纽约那栋弯曲变形的建筑免于部分坍塌。

vocabulary.en: a strong negative reaction to a recent event or development
vocabulary.excerpt: Kylie's Meta Collaboration Sparks Backlash.
vocabulary.example_cn: 凯莉与Meta的合作引发强烈反对。

vocabulary.cn: 制造共识（指通过媒体等操控公众同意）
vocabulary.en: a concept from media theory referring to the process by which media shape public opinion to accept certain policies or ideas
vocabulary.excerpt: They are manufacturing consent.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们在制造共识。

vocabulary.cn: 残忍的；病态的；对死亡感兴趣的
vocabulary.en: showing a morbid interest in death or disaster
vocabulary.excerpt: One of the reasons why people are talking about this, political people are talking about this in a kind of ghoulish way, frankly.
vocabulary.example_cn: Salah satu alasan orang membicarakan hal ini, terus terang, politisi membicarakannya dengan cara yang tidak sehat.

vocabulary.cn: Undang-undang kursi kosong (undang-undang yang mengatur cara mengisi kursi anggota parlemen yang kosong)
vocabulary.en: a law that specifies the procedure for filling a vacant legislative seat
vocabulary.excerpt: the Kentucky Senate vacancy law, meaning what do you have to do if suddenly a Senate seat is open?
vocabulary.example_cn: Undang-undang kursi kosong Senat Kentucky, artinya jika kursi Senat tiba-tiba kosong, apa yang harus Anda lakukan?

vocabulary.en: an election held to fill a vacancy that occurs between regular elections
vocabulary.excerpt: Well, there's a special election.
vocabulary.example_cn: Ya, akan ada pemilihan khusus.

vocabulary.en: officially recognized as meeting certain standards
vocabulary.excerpt: And the seat has to be empty until a winner is certified.
vocabulary.example_cn: Kursi harus tetap kosong sampai pemenang disertifikasi.

vocabulary.en: a pretense or false show
vocabulary.excerpt: This charade can't continue.
vocabulary.example_cn: Pura-pura seperti ini tidak bisa dilanjutkan.

vocabulary.en: advanced cancer of the intestine that has spread to other parts of the body
vocabulary.excerpt: he was diagnosed with stage four intestinal cancer.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他被诊断出四期肠癌。

vocabulary.cn: 连贯的；清晰的；有逻辑的
vocabulary.en: logical and consistent; able to speak clearly
vocabulary.excerpt: Scott Jennings has backed up that he's completely fine, and he's coherent.
vocabulary.example_cn: 斯科特·詹宁斯证实他完全没事，而且思路清晰。

vocabulary.en: a lot of fuss over something trivial
vocabulary.excerpt: But I think this is much ado about nothing.
vocabulary.example_cn: 但我认为这是小题大做。

vocabulary.cn: 消除；避免；使成为不必要
vocabulary.en: to remove a need or difficulty
vocabulary.excerpt: the fact that there isn't an election somehow obviates the senator from one transparency or to doing his job.
vocabulary.example_cn: 没有选举这一事实并不能免除参议员的透明度义务或履职责任。

vocabulary.en: to control the schedule for political advantage
vocabulary.excerpt: he was someone who manipulated the calendar a lot
vocabulary.example_cn: 他是一个经常操纵日程的人。

vocabulary.en: to join a discussion or argument
vocabulary.excerpt: Meghan McCain weighed in, as well.
vocabulary.example_cn: 梅根·麦凯恩也发表了意见。

vocabulary.en: legal restrictions on the number of terms an elected official can serve
vocabulary.excerpt: I'm now an advocate of age and term limits in politics.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我现在是政治中年龄和任期限制的倡导者。

vocabulary.en: beliefs that events are secretly manipulated by powerful groups
vocabulary.excerpt: leading to these conspiracies.
vocabulary.example_cn: 导致了这些阴谋论。

vocabulary.en: kind and forgiving in judging others
vocabulary.excerpt: I think there's a charitable way to read this
vocabulary.example_cn: 我认为有一种善意的解读方式。

vocabulary.en: a period of time during which something happens or is expected to happen
vocabulary.excerpt: He works on his own time frame.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他按照自己的时间表行事。

sentences.en: We've got new video just into CNN, showing the moment Mitch McConnell was taken to the hospital a month ago.
sentences.cn: 我们刚刚收到一段新视频，显示一个月前米奇·麦康奈尔被送往医院的那一刻。
sentences.structure: 主句（We've got new video）+ 后置定语（just into CNN）+ 现在分词短语（showing...）作定语修饰video，其中包含省略that的定语从句（Mitch McConnell was taken...）修饰moment。
sentences.analysis: showing the moment...是现在分词短语作后置定语，相当于which shows...；moment后接定语从句，省略了关系副词when。

sentences.en: Neighbors saw the senator being loaded into an ambulance on a stretcher.
sentences.cn: 邻居们看到这位参议员被用担架抬上救护车。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾（Neighbors saw）+ 宾语补足语（the senator being loaded...），其中being loaded是现在分词被动形式。
sentences.analysis: see sb doing sth表示看到某人正在做某事，这里用被动形式being loaded强调动作的承受者。

sentences.en: One of the reasons why people are talking about this, political people are talking about this in a kind of ghoulish way, frankly, is that the Kentucky Senate vacancy law, meaning what do you have to do if suddenly a Senate seat is open?
sentences.cn: 人们谈论此事的原因之一 --  -- 政治人物以一种近乎病态的方式谈论此事，坦率地说 --  -- 是肯塔基州参议院空缺法，即如果参议院席位突然空缺，你必须做什么？
sentences.structure: 主句（One of the reasons...is that...），其中reasons后接why引导的定语从句，插入语（political people...frankly）分隔主谓，that引导表语从句，表语从句中meaning...是现在分词短语作解释。
sentences.analysis: why引导定语从句修饰reasons；插入语增加口语化色彩；meaning...相当于which means...，对law进行解释。

sentences.en: The idea is that the clock is somehow ticking on if, suddenly, there's an open seat in Kentucky, whether it can be filled.
sentences.cn: 这个想法是，如果肯塔基州突然出现一个空缺席位，时钟就在滴答作响，无论它能否被填补。
sentences.structure: 主句（The idea is that...），that引导表语从句，表语从句中包含if引导的条件状语从句和whether引导的让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: the clock is ticking是比喻，表示时间紧迫；whether引导让步状语从句，意为"无论是否"。

sentences.en: What I don't know is why Republicans are kind of leading the charge online, talking about this.
sentences.cn: 我不知道的是，为什么共和党人似乎在网上带头讨论这件事。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What I don't know）+ 系动词is + 表语从句（why Republicans...），现在分词短语（talking about this）作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: What引导主语从句；why引导表语从句；leading the charge是习语，意为"带头冲锋"；kind of表示"有点，似乎"。

sentences.en: If McConnell is in as bad a shape as Biden ever was, or worse, he needs to step aside.
sentences.cn: 如果麦康奈尔的身体状况和拜登一样糟糕，甚至更糟，他就需要让位。
sentences.structure: If引导条件状语从句，从句中包含as...as比较结构，or worse是插入语；主句he needs to step aside。
sentences.analysis: as bad a shape as...是as...as比较结构，注意冠词位置：as + adj. + a/an + n. + as；step aside意为"让位，辞职"。

sentences.en: This charade can't continue.
sentences.cn: 这种装模作样不能再继续了。
sentences.structure: 简单主谓结构。
sentences.analysis: charade原指"哑剧字谜游戏"，这里比喻"虚伪的表演或伪装"。

sentences.en: We can't demand of others what we won't demand of ourselves.
sentences.cn: 我们不能要求别人做我们自己都不愿要求自己的事。
sentences.structure: 主句（We can't demand of others）+ 宾语从句（what we won't demand of ourselves），what在从句中作demand的宾语。
sentences.analysis: demand of sb sth是固定搭配，意为"向某人要求某物"；what引导宾语从句，相当于the thing that。

sentences.en: When my dad was running for Congress in Iowa, Western Iowa, in 2020, he was diagnosed with stage four intestinal cancer.
sentences.cn: 2020年，我父亲在爱荷华州西部竞选国会议员时，被诊断出患有四期肠癌。
sentences.structure: When引导时间状语从句，主句he was diagnosed with...。
sentences.analysis: be diagnosed with...意为"被诊断患有..."；stage four表示癌症第四期。

sentences.en: And we made the decision that, since it was an ongoing election, to come public and disclose everything.
sentences.cn: 我们决定，由于这是一场正在进行的选举，要公开并披露一切。
sentences.structure: 主句（we made the decision）+ 同位语从句（that...to come public...），从句中包含since引导的原因状语从句。
sentences.analysis: that引导同位语从句说明decision的内容；come public意为"公开"；disclose意为"披露"。

sentences.en: But you look at what's happening in the Senate right now.
sentences.cn: 但你看一下现在参议院正在发生的事情。
sentences.structure: 祈使句（you look at）+ 宾语从句（what's happening...）。
sentences.analysis: what引导宾语从句，在从句中作主语。

sentences.en: He's announced his retirement.
sentences.cn: 他已经宣布退休。
sentences.structure: 现在完成时简单句。
sentences.analysis: announce one's retirement是常见表达。

sentences.en: He'll come out when he is -- when he feels comfortable.
sentences.cn: 他会在 --  -- 在他觉得舒服的时候出来。
sentences.structure: 主句（He'll come out）+ when引导的时间状语从句，中间有插入语和重复。
sentences.analysis: come out这里指"公开露面"；when引导时间状语从句。

sentences.en: If you're a resident of the state of Kentucky and need help getting a passport with the State Department, who do you call?
sentences.cn: 如果你是肯塔基州居民，需要国务院帮助办理护照，你给谁打电话？
sentences.structure: If引导条件状语从句（包含两个并列谓语are和need），主句是特殊疑问句who do you call。
sentences.analysis: need help doing sth是固定搭配，意为"需要帮助做某事"；the State Department指美国国务院。

sentences.en: I just think the notion that, because there is not a major piece of fighting legislation that's a priority to Republicans, that somehow, the residents of the state of Kentucky aren't entitled to know, No. 1, what the health status is of one of the most senior senators in the United States.
sentences.cn: 我只是认为，因为没有一个对共和党来说优先的重大立法，就认为肯塔基州居民无权知道 --  -- 第一，美国最资深参议员之一的健康状况 --  -- 这种想法是错误的。
sentences.structure: 主句（I just think）+ 宾语从句（the notion that...），that引导同位语从句修饰notion，同位语从句中包含because引导的原因状语从句和that引导的主语从句（that...the residents...aren't entitled to know...），know后接what引导的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: notion that...是同位语从句；aren't entitled to do意为"无权做"；No. 1用于列举。

sentences.en: The fact that there isn't an election somehow obviates the senator from one transparency or to doing his job.
sentences.cn: 没有选举这一事实不知何故免除了参议员的透明度或履行其职责的义务。
sentences.structure: 主语（The fact）+ 同位语从句（that there isn't an election）+ 谓语（obviates）+ 宾语（the senator）+ 介词短语（from...）。
sentences.analysis: that引导同位语从句修饰fact；obviate sb from sth意为"使某人免除某事"；transparency这里指"透明度义务"。

sentences.en: Meghan McCain weighed in, as well.
sentences.cn: 梅根·麦凯恩也加入了讨论。
sentences.structure: Struktur subjek-predikat sederhana.
sentences.analysis: weigh in adalah idiom yang berarti 'berpartisipasi dalam diskusi atau memberikan pendapat'.

sentences.en: My family should have forced him to step down the second he was diagnosed, which is why I'm now an advocate of age and term limits in politics.
sentences.cn: Keluarga saya seharusnya memaksanya turun pada saat dia didiagnosis, itulah mengapa saya sekarang menjadi pendukung batasan usia dan masa jabatan dalam politik.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama (My family should have forced...) + klausa waktu yang dipimpin oleh the second (he was diagnosed) + klausa relatif non-restriktif yang dipimpin oleh which yang memodifikasi seluruh klausa utama.
sentences.analysis: should have done berarti seharusnya sudah dilakukan tetapi tidak; the second berarti segera setelah; step down berarti 'turun jabatan'; which merujuk pada seluruh situasi sebelumnya.

sentences.en: And I do take the point that, at this point, if Mitch McConnell is well enough to be gabbing to Scott Jennings, he should be well enough to call in to CNN or to 'The New York Times' or to FOX News or whatever, to show that he's OK.
sentences.cn: Saya memang menerima pendapat bahwa pada titik ini, jika Mitch McConnell cukup sehat untuk mengobrol dengan Scott Jennings, dia seharusnya cukup sehat untuk menelepon CNN atau New York Times atau Fox News atau media lain untuk membuktikan bahwa dia baik-baik saja.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama (I do take the point) + klausa apositif (that...), di dalamnya terdapat klausa kondisional yang dipimpin oleh if, klausa utama he should be well enough..., infinitif to show... sebagai keterangan tujuan.
sentences.analysis: do digunakan untuk penekanan; take the point berarti 'menerima pendapat'; gabbing adalah kata informal yang berarti 'mengobrol'; call in to... berarti 'menelepon...'.

sentences.en: And the fact that he hasn't done that is quite -- quite frankly, leading to these conspiracies.
sentences.cn: Fakta bahwa dia tidak melakukan itu -- terus terang -- menyebabkan teori konspirasi ini.
sentences.structure: Subjek (The fact) + klausa apositif (that he hasn't done that) + kata kerja penghubung is + predikat (leading to...), diselingi oleh sisipan quite frankly.
sentences.analysis: that memimpin klausa apositif; lead to berarti 'menyebabkan'; conspiracies di sini berarti 'teori konspirasi'.

topics.title: Kesehatan McConnell dan Transparansi Politik
topics.content: Mantan Pemimpin Mayoritas Senat AS Mitch McConnell dibawa dengan tandu ke ambulans pada 14 Juni 2026, dan video eksklusif CNN memicu spekulasi tentang kesehatannya. Rekan-rekan Partai Republik mengatakan tidak tahu apakah dia hidup atau mati, sementara undang-undang kekosongan kursi Kentucky (vacancy law) menetapkan bahwa jika kursi kosong, gubernur harus mengadakan pemilihan khusus (special election). Insiden ini menyoroti masalah transparansi (transparency) kesehatan tokoh politik, mirip dengan kontroversi sebelumnya ketika Menteri Pertahanan Austin dirawat di rumah sakit tanpa diumumkan.
topics.keywords: Mitch McConnell · transparency · vacancy law · special election

topics.title: Rencana Pembunuhan Trump oleh Iran dan Kerja Sama Intelijen AS-Israel
topics.content: Israel berbagi intelijen (intel) dengan AS bahwa Iran berencana membunuh (assassinate) mantan Presiden Trump. Berita ini mungkin menjelaskan pergantian pesawat mendadak Trump (plane switcheroo) dan alasan militer AS kembali menyerang Iran. Kerja sama intelijen AS-Israel adalah kunci keamanan Timur Tengah, dan ancaman pembunuhan sering menyebabkan peningkatan keamanan.

topics.title: Perbaikan Darurat Gedung Berbahaya di New York dan Keselamatan Bangunan
topics.content: Sebuah gedung di New York mengalami tekuk (buckling) dan berisiko runtuh sebagian (partial collapse). Perbaikan darurat sedang berlangsung, tetapi muncul pertanyaan apakah jadwal (timeline) terlalu optimis. Insiden semacam ini sering melibatkan penuaan bangunan, penilaian struktur, dan keselamatan publik.
topics.keywords: buckling · partial collapse · timeline · building safety

topics.title: Kylie Jenner Dukung Kacamata AI Meta dan Kontroversi Privasi
topics.content: Bintang reality show Kylie Jenner menjadi duta kacamata AI baru Meta, memicu reaksi keras (backlash). Kritikus menuduh Meta menggunakan ketenaran untuk menutupi kekhawatiran privasi, dan menyebut langkah ini sebagai 'memproduksi persetujuan' (manufacturing consent), yaitu memanipulasi publik melalui media untuk menerima produk kontroversial.
topics.keywords: Kylie Jenner · Meta · AI glasses · backlash · manufacturing consent

quiz.question: Dalam kalimat asli 'Israel shares intel of an Iran plant -- plan to assassinate President Trump.', apa arti 'intel'?
quiz.explanation: intel 是 intelligence 的缩写，在新闻中常指'情报'，尤其涉及军事或政治机密。此处指以色列分享伊朗暗杀计划的情报。

quiz.question: 原文 'They are manufacturing consent.' 中 'manufacturing consent' 是？
quiz.explanation: manufacturing consent 是传播学概念，指媒体通过选择性报道塑造公众对特定政策的接受，常译为'制造共识'。

quiz.question: 原文 'This charade can't continue.' 中 'charade' 的意思是？
quiz.explanation: charade 本义是'猜词游戏'，引申为'虚假的举动、装模作样'。此处指麦康奈尔健康问题的掩饰行为。

quiz.question: 根据报道，共和党议员对麦康奈尔健康状况的态度是？
quiz.explanation: 报道中多位共和党议员表示未与麦康奈尔直接沟通，甚至有人称'不知其生死'，说明他们被蒙在鼓里。

quiz.question: 以色列分享伊朗情报可能解释了以下哪件事？
quiz.explanation: 报道中明确提问'Does that explain the plane switcheroo and the renewed strikes by the U.S.?'，即伊朗暗杀计划可能解释特朗普飞机更换。

quiz.question: 批评者反对凯莉·詹娜代言Meta AI眼镜的主要原因是？
options: C. 担心隐私问题被掩盖
quiz.explanation: 报道指出批评者认为Meta利用名人效应'制造共识'，掩盖隐私担忧，即'sweep privacy concerns under the rug'。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.text: People in California, but not in a good way. This peach truck, it lost some of its fruit on the roadway and caused a major traffic jam. The peaches were eventually pushed to the median, and the lane was thankfully reopened. As if there's not enough that L.A. drivers have to deal with. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 加州的人们，但可不是什么好事。这辆桃子卡车在道路上掉落了一些水果，造成了严重的交通堵塞。桃子最终被推到中央隔离带，车道也 thankfully 重新开放了。好像洛杉矶司机要应付的事情还不够多似的。CNN头条快讯到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥蒂·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. We've got new video just into CNN, showing the moment Mitch McConnell was taken to the hospital a month ago. It's bringing up questions about transparency surrounding his health.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。我们刚刚收到一段新的视频，显示米奇·麦康奈尔一个月前被送往医院的那一刻。这引发了关于其健康状况透明度的质疑。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: He's obviously not doing well, but don't know if he's alive or has passed away.
paragraphs.cn: 他显然状况不佳，但不知道他是活着还是已经去世了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And Israel shares intel of an Iran plant -- plan to assassinate President Trump. Does that explain the plane switcheroo and the renewed strikes by the U.S.? Urgent work continues to save that buckling building in New York from partial collapse. Is the timeline too ambitious? And from hot girl summer to hot surveillance summer. Is Meta trying to sweep privacy concerns under the rug by making Kylie Jenner the face of its new A.I. glasses?
paragraphs.cn: 以色列分享了伊朗工厂的情报 --  -- 计划暗杀特朗普总统。这是否解释了飞机调换和美国重新发动空袭的原因？拯救纽约那栋摇摇欲坠的建筑免于部分倒塌的紧急工作仍在继续。时间表是否过于雄心勃勃？从辣妹夏天到监控夏天。Meta是否试图通过让凯莉·詹娜成为其新AI眼镜的代言人来掩盖隐私问题？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Do not buy the Kylie Jenner Meta glasses. Don't buy them. Point blank, period. They are manufacturing consent.
paragraphs.cn: 不要买凯莉·詹娜的Meta眼镜。不要买。就这么简单，句号。他们是在制造同意。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, this is new and exclusive video just into CNN. It's showing the moment a month ago that Senator Mitch McConnell was loaded into an ambulance. Now, his current health status remains in question. Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish, and we want to start with the discussion about the former Senate leader's health. CNN has exclusively obtained video of the moment that the Kentucky Republican was taken by ambulance from his home. This happened on June 14.
paragraphs.cn: 这是刚刚传入CNN的独家新视频。它显示了一个月前参议员米奇·麦康奈尔被抬上救护车的时刻。现在，他的当前健康状况仍存疑问。大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什，我们首先讨论前参议院领袖的健康问题。CNN独家获得了这段视频，显示这位肯塔基州共和党人在6月14日被救护车从家中接走。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Neighbors saw the senator being loaded into an ambulance on a stretcher. Two ambulances, a fire truck, and Capitol police officers were also seen blocking off the street. Now, the face of the person isn't visible in the video, but one neighbor told CNN that an eyewitness saw McConnell's face. Now, this is new detail around McConnell's hospitalization, which has been a mystery so far. Many Republican lawmakers on Capitol Hill say they've been kept in the dark. REP. BRETT GUTHRIE (R-KY): We haven't spoken directly.
paragraphs.cn: 邻居们看到这位参议员被用担架抬上救护车。还看到两辆救护车、一辆消防车和国会警察封锁了街道。视频中看不到当事人的脸，但一位邻居告诉CNN，一名目击者看到了麦康奈尔的脸。这是关于麦康奈尔住院的新细节，此前一直是个谜。国会山的许多共和党议员表示他们被蒙在鼓里。众议员布雷特·格思里（肯塔基州共和党人）：我们没有直接交谈过。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I've text with him, and I've talked to people with his office and his staff. And, you know, I don't know much more than what the public knows. SEN. MARSHA BLACKBURN (R-TN): I have not had any conversations with him. I understand that some of my colleagues have. We wish him a speedy recovery. REP. MARLIN STUTZMAN (R-IN): Yes. No, no, I have not heard from him. And I think it's a question that Republicans should be asking.
paragraphs.cn: 我和他发过短信，也和他在办公室的工作人员谈过。你知道，我知道的不比公众多多少。参议员玛莎·布莱克本（田纳西州共和党人）：我没有和他有过任何交谈。我知道我的一些同事有。我们祝愿他早日康复。众议员马林·斯图茨曼（印第安纳州共和党人）：是的。不，不，我没有收到他的消息。我认为这是共和党人应该提出的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Do you know that he's alive?
paragraphs.cn: 你知道他还活着吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Stutzman
paragraphs.text: I don't. You know, just the things that I've heard and seen from some friends is that he's obviously not doing well, but don't know if he's alive or has passed away.
paragraphs.cn: 我不知道。你知道，我从一些朋友那里听到和看到的是，他显然状况不佳，但不知道他是活着还是已经去世了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, joining me now in the group chat, Elliot Williams, CNN legal analyst and former federal prosecutor; Terry Schilling, president of the American Principles Project; and Lulu Garcia-Navarro, CNN contributor and "New York Times" journalist. One of the reasons why people are talking about this, political people are talking about this in a kind of ghoulish way, frankly, is that the Kentucky Senate vacancy law, meaning what do you have to do if suddenly a Senate seat is open? Well, there's a special election.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，加入我们群聊的有：CNN法律分析师、前联邦检察官埃利奥特·威廉姆斯；美国原则项目主席特里·席林；以及CNN撰稿人、《纽约时报》记者露露·加西亚-纳瓦罗。人们 --  -- 政治人物 --  -- 以一种近乎病态的方式谈论此事的原因之一，坦率地说，是肯塔基州的参议院席位空缺法，即如果参议院席位突然空缺，你需要做什么？嗯，会举行一场特别选举。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The governor has to call for it. And the seat has to be empty until a winner is certified. So, the idea is that the clock is somehow ticking on if, suddenly. there's an open seat in Kentucky, whether it can be filled. What I don't know is why Republicans are kind of leading the charge online, talking about this. This is Nancy Mace in a social media post: "If McConnell is in as bad a shape as Biden ever was, or worse, he needs to step aside. This charade can't continue.
paragraphs.cn: 州长必须召集选举。在获胜者得到确认之前，该席位必须保持空缺。所以，想法是，如果肯塔基州突然出现一个空缺席位，时间在滴答作响，看它能否被填补。我不知道的是，为什么共和党人在网上带头谈论这件事。这是南希·梅斯在社交媒体上发的帖子："如果麦康奈尔的状态和拜登一样糟糕，甚至更糟，他就应该让位。这场闹剧不能再继续下去了。"

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We can't demand of others what we won't demand of ourselves."
paragraphs.cn: "我们不能要求别人做到我们自己都做不到的事情。"

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.text: I think this is all getting kind of crazy, frankly. I can speak a little bit to this personally. When my dad was running for Congress in Iowa, Western Iowa, in 2020, he was diagnosed with stage four intestinal cancer. And we made the decision that, since it was an ongoing election, to come public and disclose everything. The reality is, is that I think McConnell is doing fine. Scott Jennings has backed up that he's completely fine, and he's coherent.
paragraphs.cn: 坦率地说，我认为这一切都变得有点疯狂了。我可以稍微谈一点个人经历。2020年，当我父亲在爱荷华州西部竞选国会议员时，他被诊断出患有四期肠癌。我们决定，由于选举正在进行，我们公开并披露了一切。事实是，我认为麦康奈尔做得很好。斯科特·詹宁斯证实他完全没问题，而且他思路清晰。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But don't skip over that. You said, "We made the decision to be public."
paragraphs.cn: 但别跳过这一点。你说，"我们决定公开。"

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Because there was a pending election that voters were going to make a decision on. But you look at what's happening in the Senate right now. They're not doing anything of substance. There's -- there's not going to be a SAVE Vote Act. It looks like they don't need his vote right now. He's announced his retirement. He's being replaced by, most likely, Andy Barr in Kentucky. It will be exciting to see. But I think this is much ado about nothing. I think we should let -- give him the grace and give him some privacy.
paragraphs.cn: 因为当时有一场即将到来的选举，选民们要做出决定。但看看现在参议院正在发生的事情。他们没有做任何实质性的事情。不会有《SAVE投票法案》。看起来他们现在不需要他的投票。他已经宣布退休。他很可能会被肯塔基州的安迪·巴尔取代。这将令人兴奋地看到。但我认为这完全是小题大做。我认为我们应该给予他宽容和隐私。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: He'll come out when he is -- when he feels comfortable.
paragraphs.cn: 他会在感到舒适的时候出来。

paragraphs.speaker: Elliot Williams
paragraphs.text: Can I ask a question? If you're a resident of the state of Kentucky and need help getting a passport with the State Department, who do you call?
paragraphs.cn: 我能问个问题吗？如果你是肯塔基州的居民，需要就护照问题联系国务院，你会打电话给谁？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: You call Senator Mitch McConnell's office. You -- oh, please, the Senate staff. Everyone knows that the Senate staff is really where the magic happens and makes it all work.
paragraphs.cn: 你会打给参议员米奇·麦康奈尔的办公室。你 --  -- 哦，拜托，是参议院工作人员。每个人都知道，参议院工作人员才是真正创造奇迹、让一切运转的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: I think. Well, no, no, but I just -- I just think the notion that, because there is not a major piece of --
paragraphs.cn: 我想。嗯，不，不，但我只是 --  -- 我只是认为，因为没有一项重大法案 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Fighting legislation.
paragraphs.cn: 对抗立法。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Fighting legislation that's a priority to Republicans, that somehow, the residents of the state of Kentucky aren't entitled to -- aren't entitled to know, No. 1, what the health status is of one of the most senior senators in the United States.
paragraphs.cn: 对抗共和党优先考虑的立法，不知何故，肯塔基州居民无权知道 --  -- 首先，无权知道美国最资深参议员之一的健康状况。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: And two, the fact that there isn't an election somehow obviates the senator from one transparency or to doing his job.
paragraphs.cn: 其次，没有选举这一事实不知何故免除了参议员的透明度或履行其职责。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: There's probably a trust issue, too, because I think during -- when McConnell was a leader, he was someone who manipulated the calendar a lot, somebody who would delay votes for this reason or that reason, most famously, when it came to the Supreme Court. Meghan McCain weighed in, as well. Another child of a politico, saying, "I also have a lot of regrets almost a decade since my dad's passing.
paragraphs.cn: Mungkin juga ada masalah kepercayaan, karena saya pikir selama kepemimpinan McConnell, dia sering memanipulasi agenda, menunda pemungutan suara dengan berbagai alasan, yang paling terkenal adalah masalah Mahkamah Agung. Meghan McCain juga berkomentar. Anak dari politisi lain berkata: 'Hampir sepuluh tahun sejak ayah saya meninggal, saya juga memiliki banyak penyesalan. Keluarga saya seharusnya memaksanya turun begitu dia didiagnosis, itulah mengapa saya sekarang mendukung batasan usia dan masa jabatan dalam politik. Saya juga sangat berharap Senator McConnell baik-baik saja.' Yang membuat saya terkesan adalah kata 'memaksa'.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: My family should have forced him to step down the second he was diagnosed, which is why I'm now an advocate of age and term limits in politics. I also sincerely hope Senator McConnell, OK." What struck me there was the word "forced."
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Ini dua hal. Anda memiliki pengalaman urusan politik dan urusan pribadi. Sebagai urusan pribadi, setiap orang pernah menangani orang tua yang menua, Anda harus mengambil kunci mobil mereka --

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: Yes. This is two things. And you have the experience of having it be both a political matter and a personal matter. Everyone, as a personal matter, has had to deal with aging parents who you have to take their car keys away and --
paragraphs.cn: -- dan melakukan percakapan sulit itu. Mereka harus memutuskan apa yang akan mereka lakukan, seperti apakah akan masuk ke fasilitas khusus, dll. Ketika ini bukan hanya masalah pemilihan, tetapi masalah transparansi, itu menjadi urusan politik.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: -- and have that difficult conversation. They have to decide what they're going to do when they -- you know, if they're going to go into special facilities, et cetera, et cetera. When it becomes more than that, it's when it's a political matter and not just about the election, but about transparency.
paragraphs.cn: Saya menerima itu, pada titik ini, jika Mitch McConnell cukup sehat untuk mengobrol dengan Scott Jennings, maka dia seharusnya juga bisa menelepon CNN atau New York Times atau Fox News, dll., untuk menunjukkan bahwa dia baik-baik saja. Dan dia tidak melakukannya, terus terang, ini menyebabkan teori konspirasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: And I do take the point that, at this point, if -- if Mitch McConnell is well enough to be gabbing to Scott Jennings, he should be well enough to call in to CNN or to "The New York Times" or to FOX News or whatever, to -- to show that he's OK. And the fact that he hasn't done that is quite -- quite frankly, leading to these conspiracies.
paragraphs.cn: Jika saya boleh, karena saya pikir ada cara interpretasi yang baik, terutama jika Anda mengenal Mitch McConnell. Dia adalah orang yang sangat kuat. Dia tidak suka diberi tahu apa yang harus dilakukan, terutama tidak suka instruksi dari tekanan publik.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: If I could, because, again, I think there's a charitable way to read this, especially if you know Mitch McConnell. He is a very strong man. He does not like being told what to do, especially by public pressure.
paragraphs.cn: Dia berjalan sesuai jadwalnya sendiri. Jadi, saya pikir Anda harus memberinya waktu. Beri dia beberapa hari, tarik napas dalam-dalam.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: He -- he works on his own time frame. And so, I think you've got to give it to him. Give him a few days, take it -- take a deep breath.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But let me play for someone you pointed out this week who you could describe very similarly, the former defense secretary, Lloyd Austin. When he went to the hospital for his treatment, it became quite the scandal for Republicans. And in the end, he came out, more or less kind of apologized. And I want to play for you the lesson that he took away from that moment.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi biarkan saya memutar untuk Anda sesuatu tentang seseorang yang Anda sebut minggu ini, dan Anda bisa menggambarkannya dengan cara yang sangat mirip, yaitu mantan Menteri Pertahanan Lloyd Austin. Ketika dia pergi ke rumah sakit untuk perawatan, ini menyebabkan skandal yang cukup besar di kalangan Republikan. Akhirnya, dia tampil dan kurang lebih meminta maaf. Saya ingin Anda mendengar pelajaran yang dia ambil dari momen itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Lloyd Austin
paragraphs.text: I don't think it's news that I'm a pretty private guy. I never liked burdening others with my problems. It's just not my way. But I've learned from this experience, taking this kind of job means losing some of the privacy that most of us expect. The American people have a right to know if their leaders are facing health challenges that might affect their ability to perform their duties, even temporarily.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir saya cukup pribadi, itu bukan berita. Saya tidak pernah suka membebani orang lain dengan masalah saya sendiri. Itu bukan gaya saya. Tapi melalui pengalaman ini, saya belajar bahwa memiliki pekerjaan seperti ini berarti kehilangan beberapa privasi yang kita harapkan. Rakyat Amerika berhak tahu apakah pemimpin mereka menghadapi tantangan kesehatan yang mungkin mempengaruhi kemampuan mereka untuk menjalankan tugas, bahkan jika sementara.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: "Even temporarily." Also notice, he said, the loss of privacy is part of the gig. And I just want to put up some images here, in memoriam. These are lawmakers who have died while they were holding office since 2024. And you pointed out many of these names.
paragraphs.cn: 'Bahkan jika sementara.' Perhatikan juga, dia mengatakan kehilangan privasi adalah bagian dari pekerjaan ini. Saya hanya ingin menunjukkan beberapa gambar di sini, sebagai penghormatan. Ini adalah anggota parlemen yang meninggal saat menjabat sejak 2024. Anda menyebut banyak nama itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: A lot of Dems up there.
paragraphs.cn: Ada banyak Demokrat di atas.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Well, it's a lot of old people.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, ada banyak orang tua.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: I mean, I think let's just -- We should also discuss the fact that the United States Senate is a gerontocracy. It is. Well, it is.
paragraphs.cn: Maksud saya, saya pikir kita harus mendiskusikan fakta bahwa Senat AS adalah gerontokrasi. Memang benar. Ya, memang benar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I think the reason why people are discussing is because the Biden thing --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir alasan orang membahasnya adalah karena masalah Biden --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- was like a big wakeup call where everybody was questioning, and Republicans are still questioning.
paragraphs.cn: -- seperti lonceng peringatan besar, semua orang mempertanyakan, dan Republikan masih mempertanyakan.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: Can we talk about the Trump thing? I mean, this is the other thing.
paragraphs.cn: Bisakah kita bicara tentang masalah Trump? Maksud saya, ini hal lain.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: We don't know what President Trump actually is suffering from. What are his swelling --
paragraphs.cn: Kita tidak tahu penyakit apa yang sebenarnya diderita Presiden Trump. Apa bengkaknya --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: What are you talking about?
paragraphs.cn: Apa yang kamu bicarakan?

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: Well, there's a lot of --
paragraphs.cn: Yah, ada banyak --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: There's -- we have done many segments on the rumors about various health issues.
paragraphs.cn: Ada -- kami telah melakukan banyak liputan tentang rumor berbagai masalah kesehatan.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: About various health issues where doctors speculated, with has swollen ankles, you know, the continued bruising on his arms, like -- on his hands. I mean, there are lots of questions about, when -- when people get to a certain age. I mean, let's be honest. I mean, he's 80. Mitch McConnell is -- how old is Mitch McConnell now?
paragraphs.cn: Tentang berbagai masalah kesehatan, dokter berspekulasi, seperti pembengkakan pergelangan kaki, dan memar terus-menerus di lengannya, juga di tangannya. Maksud saya, ketika orang mencapai usia tertentu, ada banyak pertanyaan. Sejujurnya, dia 80 tahun. Mitch McConnell -- berapa umur Mitch McConnell sekarang?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: He should be in his 80s. I don't think --
paragraphs.cn: Dia seharusnya berusia 80-an. Saya tidak berpikir --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: Yes, he's in his 80s. I mean, obviously, Biden was also in his 80s. I mean, you start to have serious health concerns. And my point about this, more broadly, is I think the more general point, which is about transparency and what do you owe the voting public about --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, dia 80-an. Jelas, Biden juga 80-an. Maksud saya, Anda akan mulai memiliki kekhawatiran kesehatan yang serius. Pandangan saya yang lebih luas adalah, saya pikir masalah yang lebih umum adalah transparansi, dan apa yang harus Anda ungkapkan kepada publik pemilih --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: What do you owe the voting public about your health?
paragraphs.cn: Tentang kesehatan Anda, apa yang harus Anda ungkapkan kepada publik pemilih?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And here's a fact I owe all of you. He's 84.
paragraphs.cn: Dan saya akan memberi tahu Anda satu fakta: dia berusia 84 tahun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But I have to be honest. One of the things that happened with Biden and Trump was this idea of, like, well, there's a vigorous and, you know, still operating 80-something. And then there's, like, a deteriorating something. And I think what the audience and voters want to know is, are we all on the same page about when it's OK not to say anything --
paragraphs.cn: But honestly, one thing that happens with Biden and Trump is that there's a view that some people in their 80s are energetic and still functioning, while others are in decline. I think what the audience and voters want to know is whether we all agree on when not to say something -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- about what's happening? And the answer is sounding more and more like no.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- about what's happening? And the answer increasingly seems to be: no.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Well, yes. And moreover, I think a lot of people showed their behinds about Bidens age. I mean, I think we went through -- people don't remember, there was a year-long debate about Joe Biden being unfit for the presidency.
paragraphs.cn: Well, yes. And I think many people have exposed their ignorance on the issue of Biden's age. I mean, we went through a year-long debate about whether Joe Biden is fit to be president.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Which was right for us to have as a nation, because it was clear the president was not fit. This is an individual who is, No. 1, one of the senior leaders on the Appropriations Committee, a former Senate majority leader and a representative serving the state of Kentucky. And I think it's a reasonable question to ask, is this individual fit to serve? And are all the people who are out there vouching for his health creating problems for themselves?
paragraphs.cn: As a country, it's right for us to have that debate because it's clear the president is not fit. This person, first of all, is one of the senior leaders of the Appropriations Committee, a former Senate Majority Leader, and a congressman from Kentucky. I think it's reasonable to ask: is this person fit to serve? Are all those who endorsed his health setting themselves up for trouble?

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Later on, by being on the record about it?
paragraphs.cn: Later, by publicly recording these remarks?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me follow up on something else quite serious. Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, is Iran looking to assassinate President Trump? This was the warning from Israel to tip off the White House. Plus, extreme heat fueling dangerous and deadly wildfires in Spain. And where will LeBron go? The odds on Kalshi say there's a greater than 60 percent chance he heads to Cleveland, followed by the Heat and Warriors. We're going to tell you what Charles Barkley thinks.
paragraphs.cn: Let me follow up on another very serious topic. On CNN's This Morning, is Iran plotting to assassinate President Trump? This is a warning Israel sent to the White House. Also, extreme heat has sparked dangerous and deadly wildfires in Spain. And, where will LeBron go? Kalshi odds show he has over a 60% chance of going to Cleveland, followed by the Heat and the Warriors. We'll tell you what Charles Barkley thinks.

paragraphs.speaker: Charles Barkley
paragraphs.text: He should go back to Cleveland and finish his career there. If he goes to Philly, there's people going to say he's ring-chasing to catch Michael. If he goes to Golden State, they're going to say he's ring- chasing.
paragraphs.cn: He should go back to Cleveland and end his career there. If he goes to Philadelphia, people will say he's chasing rings to catch Michael. If he goes to Golden State, they'll also say he's chasing rings.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, an alleged plot by Iran to assassinate President Trump, uncovered by Israel. Sources tell CNN they shared the intelligence with the United States this week.
paragraphs.cn: So, Iran's alleged plot to assassinate President Trump was discovered by Israel. Sources tell CNN they shared this intelligence with the US this week.

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump

All right, straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to turn to this news. The threats from Mexico to take the U.S. to court over the deaths of ICE detainees. And could a foreign government succeed where U.S.
paragraphs.cn: What do young people say, the propaganda they are unsubscribing from? Ali Shapiro, thank you very much. You can now see us in the latest episode of 'The Participation Party'. New episodes air every Friday. We discussed this issue, and the FROYO movement. Very interesting. Thank you very much, Ali. Okay, next on 'CNN This Morning', we will focus on this news: Mexico threatens to take the US to court over ICE detainee death. Can foreign governments succeed where US advocates have failed?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: advocates have failed? Plus, personality over substance. Voters getting candidates who are actually qualified for the job. Maybe. Maybe not.
paragraphs.cn: Also, personality over substance. Voters are getting candidates truly qualified for the job. Maybe. Maybe not.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, the 52-year-old Mexican man killed by ICE in Houston this week was not the target of an immigration operation. That's according to a source. Texas authorities previously notified ICE about two people believed to be in the U.S. illegally, traveling in a white van. And neither of those men were the victim. You're looking at video from the aftermath of the shooting. I have to warn you, it is disturbing because you can see a bloodied person on the ground.
paragraphs.cn: The 52-year-old Mexican man killed by ICE in Houston this week was not the target of the immigration operation. According to sources, Texas authorities previously notified ICE that two people believed to be illegally in the US were traveling in a white van. Neither of these two was the victim. What you see is video after the shooting. I must warn you, it is disturbing, because you can see a bloodied person on the ground.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, there's no official footage of the shooting that has been released. Federal officials involved were not wearing body cameras. ICE claims the man attempted to take off as agents tried to pull him over. His family is pushing back on that.
paragraphs.cn: There is no officially released footage of the shooting. The federal officer involved was not wearing a body camera. ICE claims the man tried to flee when agents attempted to stop him. His family disputes this.

paragraphs.speaker: Ronaldo Salgado
paragraphs.text: He did not know that those vehicles following him were ICE agents. Again, these vehicles were unmarked. They had no emblems, no logos, no flashing lights. There was no way for him to know that they were ICE agents. I know my dad would have stopped had ICE agents formally and clearly identified themselves, at least through their vehicle markings.
paragraphs.cn: 他不知道那些跟在他后面的车辆是ICE特工。同样，这些车没有标记。它们没有徽章、没有标志、没有闪光灯。他根本无法知道他们是ICE特工。我知道，如果ICE特工正式且明确地表明身份，至少通过车辆标识，我父亲会停车的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In the meantime, Mexico, fed up with the deaths of its people on U.S. soil. Their president is seeking a criminal investigation in the U.S. related deaths of 17 Mexican nationals during immigration operations in recent months. PRESIDENT CLAUDIA SHEINBAUM, MEXICO (through translator): What happened recently with the death of a Mexican in an ICE operation, not only is it sad and regrettable, but it seems to be targeted. So, obviously, we will maintain the diplomatic relationship.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，墨西哥对其公民在美国领土上的死亡感到厌倦。墨西哥总统正寻求在美国对近几个月移民行动中17名墨西哥国民的死亡进行刑事调查。墨西哥总统克劳迪娅·辛鲍姆（通过翻译）表示：最近一名墨西哥人在ICE行动中死亡，不仅令人悲伤和遗憾，而且似乎是有针对性的。因此，显然我们将维持外交关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But we made the decision to file a formal complaint with the state and federal prosecutor's office of the United States against those who are found responsible for what we consider to be homicides.
paragraphs.cn: 但我们决定向美国州和联邦检察官办公室提出正式投诉，追究那些我们认为构成杀人罪的责任人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Mexico's foreign minister says the country will file criminal complaints directly with U.S. prosecutors. The group chat is back. And, Elliot, we need your expertise.
paragraphs.cn: 墨西哥外长表示，该国将直接向美国检察官提起刑事诉讼。群聊又回来了。埃利奥特，我们需要你的专业知识。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Cornish
paragraphs.text: Because, in general, when you're looking at deaths of ICE detainees --
paragraphs.cn: 因为，总的来说，当你审视ICE被拘留者的死亡时 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Obviously there is a spike. As of 2025, that number up to 33. It's gone down a bit. And I think people thought maybe ICE had been operating differently, or at least more covertly on the streets of the
paragraphs.cn: 显然有一个高峰。截至2025年，这一数字上升到33。后来有所下降。我认为人们可能以为ICE的运作方式有所不同，或者至少在美国街头更加隐蔽。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You're shaking your head.
paragraphs.cn: 你在摇头。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: No. It's just covertly might be the word or just simply not in public in the same way. Now, the big picture governing all of this is that law enforcement has tremendously broad latitude. And I think latitude that would shock and alarm many people, both in terms of deaths in custody, but also in terms of their conduct in the field. Now, certainly, Mexico is free to try to, you know, engage prosecutors, file lawsuits or whatever else.
paragraphs.cn: 不。只是"隐蔽"这个词可能合适，或者干脆就是不像以前那样公开。现在，这一切的大背景是执法部门拥有极其广泛的自由裁量权。我认为这种自由裁量权会让很多人震惊和警觉，无论是在押人员死亡方面，还是他们在现场的行为方面。当然，墨西哥可以自由地尝试接触检察官、提起诉讼或其他任何方式。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: I will note, you know, the Department of Homeland Security will be defended by the Justice Department in any federal suit in the United States right now. And I just think the likelihood of any of these suits really gaining traction --
paragraphs.cn: 我要指出，目前在美国任何联邦诉讼中，国土安全部都将由司法部辩护。我只是认为这些诉讼取得进展的可能性 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Just based on who the Justice Department is right now, they have an interest in protecting the Department of Homeland Security, is pretty low, but they can. That is their right. But two other avenues. Really quick, Audie, two other avenues for proceeding, if they're serious. Number one, go to the states. Every state can entertain lawsuits, even against the federal government. You can have the attorney general or local prosecutor bring a lawsuit.
paragraphs.cn: 仅基于司法部目前的立场，他们有意保护国土安全部，所以可能性很低，但他们可以这样做。这是他们的权利。但还有另外两条途径。很快说一下，奥迪，如果他们认真对待的话，还有两条前进的途径。第一，去各州。每个州都可以受理诉讼，甚至针对联邦政府。你可以让州检察长或地方检察官提起诉讼。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: But also Congress can oversee and step in if ICE is behaving in a manner that's not appropriate.
paragraphs.cn: 但如果ICE的行为不当，国会也可以进行监督并介入。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, right now, ICE related Mexican national deaths in Trump's term, 14 people in ICE custody, three in ICE operations. Sheinbaum had more or less kind of played nice with Trump when it came to tariffs, right, and when it came to the drug war. Why do you think, Lulu, that she is speaking out now and in such a specific way, using the phrase targeted?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，目前特朗普任期内与ICE相关的墨西哥国民死亡人数为：14人在ICE拘留期间死亡，3人在ICE行动中死亡。辛鲍姆在关税和毒品战争问题上或多或少对特朗普表现出了友好态度。卢卢，你认为她为什么现在以如此具体的方式发声，并使用了"针对"这个词？

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: Yes. This is always a hugely sensitive issue for Mexico. Let's not forget that, you know, Mexico has the largest number of immigrants in the United States, and they share a border. And so, the way that ICE and Border Patrol interact with that population has always been a source of enormous sensitivity and tension.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这对墨西哥来说一直是一个非常敏感的问题。别忘了，墨西哥在美国的移民数量最多，而且两国共享边界。因此，ICE和边境巡逻队与该群体的互动方式一直是高度敏感和紧张的根源。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: And I think one of the reasons they're doing this is not only because they think they're going to get satisfaction, but it is to be perceived by their own people as doing something, as raising complaints, as trying to actively engage.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为他们这样做的一个原因不仅是他们认为会得到满意的结果，而且是为了让本国人民看到他们在采取行动、提出投诉、并积极介入。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: Because it's not only that the government is in this very difficult position with trying to appease Trump and work with him while also defending its population, but the people themselves do not like the Trump administration, do not like the way that this is playing out, and want their government to do something.
paragraphs.cn: 因为不仅政府处于非常困难的境地，既要安抚特朗普并与之合作，又要保护本国人民，而且人民本身也不喜欢特朗普政府，不喜欢事态的发展，希望他们的政府有所作为。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: And so, this is a way of them showing that they are.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，这是他们展示自己正在行动的一种方式。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And it raises the profile of ICE operations in a moment when they had effectively lowered the temperature after the mass protests in two separate cities, and deaths on the streets.
paragraphs.cn: 而在两个不同城市的大规模抗议和街头死亡事件之后，ICE实际上已经降低了紧张局势，此举又提高了ICE行动的关注度。

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.text: Yes. Well, look, I -- my message to the president of Mexico, Claudia Sheinbaum, is, game on. You know, you want to sue over this. I guess we'll have to sue over the hundreds of thousands of young men and women in this country that have died from fentanyl overdoses or even the hundreds of people that have had violence inflicted on them that have been raped and sexually assaulted. You know, Claudia Sheinbaum is one to talk. She was elected in a year where there were over 27 political assassinations in her party.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。好吧，我想对墨西哥总统克劳迪娅·辛鲍姆说：放马过来。你想就此提起诉讼。我想我们也不得不对这个国家因芬太尼过量死亡的数十万年轻人，甚至遭受暴力、强奸和性侵犯的数百人提起诉讼。克劳迪娅·辛鲍姆说得倒轻巧。她当选的那一年，她的政党内有超过27起政治暗杀事件。

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.text: She's literally a hand-picked candidate for the cartels. And again --
paragraphs.cn: 她简直就是贩毒集团精心挑选的候选人。而且 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You've levied a lot of different things there.
paragraphs.cn: 你刚才提出了很多不同的指控。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, I want to hold on because I'm not fact checking you on that level, OK. But I do have this question about, is it fair for a country to say, you're killing our nationals on your street and that this is state sanctioned deaths, and therefore.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我想先说明，我不会在这个层面上核实你的说法，好吧。但我确实有个问题：一个国家说'你们在我们的街道上杀害我们的国民，这是国家批准的死亡'，这样说公平吗？因此……

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Well, look, I noticed in that chart, in 2023 and 2024 there were -- I think in 2023 it said there were seven deaths by ICE and 11 deaths in 2024. That's when there was like no immigration enforcement at all. And it's only around 50 between these last two years. There it goes, 33 and 19 in two years. That's 52.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你看，我注意到那张图表上，2023年和2024年 --  -- 我记得2023年显示有7人死于ICE，2024年有11人。那是在几乎没有移民执法的情况下。而过去两年只有大约50人。你看，两年内33人和19人，总共52人。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: That's when we're actually deporting people and actually enforcing the law. So, it seems like there's some violence embedded into illegal immigration in the first place.
paragraphs.cn: 那是在我们实际驱逐人员、执行法律的时候。所以，似乎非法移民本身就带有一些暴力。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: I -- well, no, no, no. I -- the only thing I will say to sort of push back on that gently is, and I worked at -- I was a senior official at ICE for five years. You're still talking about deporting 400,000 people a year, even under the low points of any administration's immigration enforcement efforts. So, the idea that enforcement simply isn't happening, and those are, I believe we were talking about detention centers, people are detained, beds are full.
paragraphs.cn: 我 --  -- 嗯，不，不，不。我唯一想温和地反驳的是，我在ICE担任高级官员五年。即使在任何政府移民执法工作的低谷期，你仍然在谈论每年驱逐40万人。所以，认为执法根本没有发生的想法，以及那些 --  -- 我相信我们讨论的是拘留中心，人们被拘留，床位是满的。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: It's just not accurate that no -- that no enforcement was happening. Now, certainly we can agree, and I think we should, that more enforcement is happening now than happened in 2023. But I -- but I don't think that that -- those numbers are reflective of enforcement simply not happening in detention centers.
paragraphs.cn: Mengatakan tidak ada penegakan hukum yang terjadi adalah tidak akurat. Tentu, kita bisa setuju, dan saya pikir harus setuju, bahwa penegakan hukum sekarang lebih banyak daripada tahun 2023. Tapi saya tidak berpikir angka-angka itu mencerminkan bahwa pusat penahanan tidak memiliki penegakan hukum sama sekali.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: So, a lot of people are detained by the United States.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, banyak orang ditahan di Amerika Serikat.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: And I'll also say that they -- (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: Saya juga akan mengatakan, mereka -- -- (cross talk)

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: And I will say that the implication --
paragraphs.cn: Yang ingin saya katakan adalah, sindiran ini -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: And a lot more people were coming into the United States in those years.
paragraphs.cn: Dan pada tahun-tahun itu, lebih banyak orang masuk ke Amerika Serikat.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: Yes, but I'll also say that the implication that somehow enforcement is violent and therefore these deaths are somehow acceptable is --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, tapi saya juga akan mengatakan, sindiran bahwa penegakan hukum itu kejam, sehingga kematian-kematian ini sampai batas tertentu dapat diterima, itu -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And not accept -- well, let's pause on this. It's not acceptable, certainly, to the families. And as we started this story with somebody who's lost their father when they weren't even the target of the operation. So, I think the question of, what standard do we hold ourselves to is coming up in this. I want you guys to stay with me. We're going to turn to another story in the future talking about NASA, because NASA is now recruiting volunteers for a yearlong moon and mars simulation.
paragraphs.cn: Dan tidak dapat diterima -- -- baiklah, kita berhenti sejenak di sini. Ini tentu tidak dapat diterima bagi keluarga. Seperti yang kami sebutkan saat memulai cerita ini, ada seseorang yang kehilangan ayah, dan mereka bahkan bukan target operasi. Jadi, saya pikir pertanyaan tentang standar apa yang kita pegang sedang muncul. Harap tetap bersama kami. Selanjutnya kita akan beralih ke cerita lain tentang NASA, karena NASA sedang merekrut sukarelawan untuk misi simulasi bulan dan Mars selama satu tahun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Voters will be in space-like -- sorry, volunteers will be in space-like conditions, living inside confined habitats and carrying out tasks, just as astronauts would at the start of the year.
paragraphs.cn: Para pemilih akan berada di lingkungan seperti luar angkasa -- -- maaf, para sukarelawan akan berada di lingkungan seperti luar angkasa, tinggal di habitat tertutup, menjalankan misi, seperti yang dilakukan astronot di awal tahun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: An alleged plot by Iran to assassinate President Trump uncovered by Israel. Sources tell CNN they shared the intelligence with the U.S. this week. I'm bringing in "New York Times" White House correspondent Maggie Haberman. She's co-author of the new bestseller "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump." Maggie, thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: Israel menemukan sebuah konspirasi Iran yang diduga untuk membunuh Presiden Trump. Sumber memberi tahu CNN bahwa mereka membagikan intelijen ini dengan Amerika Serikat minggu ini. Saya mengundang jurnalis Gedung Putih New York Times, Maggie Haberman. Dia adalah rekan penulis buku baru yang laris, 'Rezim Pergantian: Di Dalam Kepresidenan Kekaisaran Trump'. Maggie, terima kasih banyak sudah datang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This seems like a good week to have this conversation between the inflection point ending the ceasefire and then this moment of this reporting we're getting now of a potential assassination attempt, information shared by Israel. This is a president who has survived assassination attempts.
paragraphs.cn: Minggu ini tampaknya menjadi waktu yang tepat untuk melakukan percakapan ini, dari berakhirnya titik balik perjanjian gencatan senjata, hingga laporan yang kita dapatkan sekarang tentang upaya pembunuhan potensial, informasi yang dibagikan oleh Israel. Ini adalah presiden yang telah selamat dari beberapa upaya pembunuhan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Does this kind of concern loom large for him? MAGGIE HABERMAN, WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENT, "THE NEW YORK TIMES": Well, there's no question that among the reasons that the president and his team were really radicalized in the interregnum period before he came back to office, and we write about this in the book, were the assassination attempts that he faced.
paragraphs.cn: Apakah kekhawatiran ini tampak sangat menonjol baginya? Jurnalis Gedung Putih New York Times, Maggie Haberman: Tidak diragukan lagi, salah satu alasan mengapa presiden dan timnya menjadi agresif selama masa transisi sebelum dia kembali ke Gedung Putih adalah upaya pembunuhan yang dia hadapi, yang juga kami tulis dalam buku.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In particular, he -- Butler happened around the time that someone had been charged with being part of a murder for hire plot involving -- or set up by Iranian officials allegedly. And in his mind, and in the campaign's mind at the time, it all became one thing. And so, there's no question that the fact that this new information about a potential plot is going to loom large for this team. It is going to raise new concerns.
paragraphs.cn: Khususnya, peristiwa Butler terjadi sebelum dan sesudah seseorang dituduh terlibat dalam konspirasi pembunuhan bayaran yang direncanakan oleh pejabat Iran. Di matanya dan tim kampanyenya saat itu, semua ini menjadi satu kesatuan. Jadi, tidak diragukan lagi, informasi baru tentang konspirasi potensial akan berdampak besar pada tim ini. Ini akan menimbulkan kekhawatiran baru.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You are already seeing a president who, Audie, does not travel outside the White House that much. Part of it is that, you know, he would tell aides at the beginning of the -- of the term, I'm done campaigning. But part of it are -- is legitimate security concerns.
paragraphs.cn: Anda sudah melihat seorang presiden, Audi, yang jarang meninggalkan Gedung Putih. Sebagian karena, Anda tahu, dia memberi tahu para pembantunya di awal masa jabatannya, saya sudah selesai berkampanye. Tapi sebagian karena pertimbangan keamanan yang wajar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's interesting talk -- hearing from you in this moment because he has such a different cast of characters around him compared to term one. How does that affect something like prosecuting this war, where we see like while people are having talks, he says, look, ceasefire is over, and all of a sudden we are in the middle of strikes again.
paragraphs.cn: Menarik, saat mendengarkan Anda saat ini, karena orang-orang di sekitarnya sangat berbeda dibandingkan dengan masa jabatan pertama. Bagaimana ini mempengaruhi hal-hal seperti memajukan perang ini? Kita melihat, ketika orang bernegosiasi, dia berkata, lihat, gencatan senjata berakhir, lalu tiba-tiba kita kembali dalam serangan udara.

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.text: So, there's a couple of things, Audie. One is that this government, unlike term one, and one of the things that we write about very clearly is how radically different this term is than the first term. The decisions are being made by a small handful of about a half a dozen advisors. And if someone is not in the room with those advisors in the government, they often have no idea what is going on.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, ada beberapa poin, Audi. Pertama, pemerintahan ini berbeda dari masa jabatan pertama, kami tulis dengan sangat jelas dalam buku betapa berbedanya masa jabatan ini dengan masa jabatan pertama. Keputusan dibuat oleh sekelompok kecil sekitar enam penasihat. Jika seseorang tidak berada di ruangan yang sama dengan para penasihat ini di pemerintahan, mereka sering tidak tahu apa yang sedang terjadi.

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.text: And that is generally by design, including Steve Witkoff, who is Trump's envoy for everything, who had been negotiating this ceasefire or some kind of an Iranian deal for some time. But we also describe how last year Steve Witkoff thought that he was getting close to a deal, and then Trump made some other pronouncement, and it became very clear that foreign policy, as someone said, you know, and we quote them anonymously in the book, is, whatever Trump says at any given moment.
paragraphs.cn: Ini sering disengaja, termasuk Steve Witkoff, utusan khusus Trump, yang telah merundingkan gencatan senjata atau semacam kesepakatan Iran untuk beberapa waktu. Tapi kami juga menggambarkan bagaimana tahun lalu Steve Witkoff mengira dia hampir mencapai kesepakatan, lalu Trump membuat pernyataan lain, dan hasilnya jelas, kebijakan luar negeri, seperti yang dikatakan seseorang, yang kami kutip secara anonim dalam buku, adalah apa pun yang dikatakan Trump pada saat tertentu.

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.text: And so, you are seeing that right now with this ceasefire where I think it is -- it is most problematic is for diplomat -- long-term diplomatic conversations, incremental comments matter, mixed signals matter. And that has obviously happened for some time here. This is also a war that the president clearly wants to be done with. And there just is no quick fix. And that is frustrating him.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, apa yang Anda lihat sekarang adalah situasi ini, dalam perjanjian gencatan senjata ini, saya pikir yang paling bermasalah adalah diplomasi -- -- dalam dialog diplomatik jangka panjang, pernyataan bertahap itu penting, sinyal campuran juga penting. Dan situasi ini jelas telah berlangsung selama beberapa waktu. Ini juga perang yang jelas ingin diakhiri oleh presiden, tapi tidak ada solusi cepat. Ini membuatnya frustrasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I want to turn to another issue because immigration has been obviously a defining issue for Trump in both terms, but arguably he's probably made the most gains in this term. Largely, Stephen Miller has looked at as the architect of that. Can you talk to me about the, where we are now, meaning Noem is gone, we were just talking about Mexico complaining about Mexican nationals being targeted. Is this something that the architects around Trump -- that it's by design?
paragraphs.cn: Saya ingin beralih ke masalah lain, karena imigrasi jelas merupakan isu khas dalam dua masa jabatan Trump, tapi bisa dibilang dia membuat kemajuan terbesar dalam masa jabatan ini. Sebagian besar, Stephen Miller dipandang sebagai arsitek kebijakan ini. Bisakah Anda berbicara dengan saya tentang situasi kita sekarang? Maksud saya, Noem sudah keluar, dan kita baru saja membahas Meksiko mengeluh bahwa warga Meksiko menjadi sasaran. Apakah ini sengaja dilakukan oleh para arsitek di sekitar Trump?

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.text: So, we have seen a lot of fits and starts with immigration efforts and policy because there was such a flood the zone effort. Stephen Miller spent several years preparing for a second Trump term. He looked at all of the levers of government. He had it -- he has an actual plan for what he wanted to do. He is more hard line on immigration than Trump is, although Trump obviously only tends to react and, you know, reel Miller in when there's negative media coverage.
paragraphs.cn: Kita melihat banyak pasang surut dalam kebijakan dan operasi imigrasi, karena ada banyak upaya masuk pada saat itu. Stephen Miller menghabiskan waktu bertahun-tahun mempersiapkan masa jabatan kedua Trump. Dia mempelajari berbagai tuas pemerintahan. Dia punya rencana nyata, tahu apa yang ingin dia lakukan. Dia lebih keras daripada Trump dalam masalah imigrasi, meskipun Trump jelas hanya bereaksi ketika liputan media negatif muncul, dan menahan Miller.

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.text: And so, you saw that earlier this year with the shooting deaths of Renee Good and Alex Pretti. And these showy ICE sweeps into cities, these, you know, dramatic displays that the administration would clip and put out in social media videos, those slowed down, but that doesn't mean that the efforts slowed down. And so, you are going -- you are seeing -- my colleagues have written about this at "The Times," larger sweeps. You are seeing -- they're just broadcast with less vigor and intensity.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, Anda melihat peristiwa penembakan Renee Good dan Alex Pretty di awal tahun ini. Dan juga penggerebekan ICE yang mencolok di kota-kota, pertunjukan dramatis yang diedit dan diunggah pemerintah ke video media sosial, operasi ini melambat, tapi itu tidak berarti upaya melambat. Jadi, Anda akan melihat -- -- rekan saya di New York Times menulis -- -- penggerebekan yang lebih besar. Anda akan melihat -- -- hanya saja intensitas publisitasnya berkurang.

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.text: It calmed the protests down at the time. I don't know where this goes now. But they are trying now -- Stephen Miller had a target of 3,000 arrests a day. That was not doable. paragraphs.cn: Itu meredakan protes saat itu. Saya tidak tahu ke mana arahnya sekarang. Tapi mereka sekarang mencoba -- -- target Stephen Miller adalah menangkap 3000 orang per hari. Itu tidak layak.
